description: Reads today's logged meals from the database and computes remaining calories / protein / fat / carbs. All suggestions are scoped to that budget.
Uses the user's configured daily macro targets to calculate remaining budget. Falls back to conservative defaults if no profile exists.
Asks the user five CLI questions: target calories / taste / max prep time / ingredients on hand / few-ingredients preference.
If the user sets a calorie target smaller than the full remaining budget all four macros are scaled proportionally so scoring stays consistent.
Sends remaining macros and preferences to an LLM which translates them into valid Spoonacular complexSearch API parameters.
If the LLM call fails the system falls back to a rule-based parameter builder that applies the same constraints deterministically.
Fetches up to 8 recipe candidates from the Spoonacular complexSearch endpoint with full nutrition data and source URLs.
Fetches recipe candidates from a local SQLite database built from a Kaggle dataset. No API key or internet connection required.
Runs both Spoonacular and Kaggle simultaneously and merges results into a single ranked list with per-source counts shown.
If the local Kaggle database does not exist it is built automatically on first run from the raw CSV (one-time ~30 seconds).
Hard-discards recipes that structurally cannot contribute enough nutrition. Discards recipes that would exceed the calorie budget by more than 20% even when scaled.
Scales recipes up to the nearest 0.25 portion (max 2.5x) when a single serving does not cover the macro thresholds.
Scores recipes on a weighted formula (protein 45% / calories 30% / carbs 15% / fat 10%) and sorts best-fit-first. Attaches a 0-100 fit score to each result.
If remaining calories are below 200 kcal the system prints a quick-options tip (protein shake / Greek yogurt / cottage cheese / eggs) before the preference prompts.
Source is selectable at runtime via --source CLI flag (spoonacular / kaggle / combo) for easy switching between online and offline operation.
Runs as a standalone module via python -m step6_recipe.recipe_suggester with a --user flag for targeting a specific user ID.
